issues.title: How to access the already running process in the container programmatically?
issues.next_action_reason: Community question with only a bot reply; answer how to reach the init process via the SDK and cross-link the stdin bug #41091.
issues.suggested_reply: Hi @Sonix — the WSL.Containers API models the init process (the image's `CMD`/`ENTRYPOINT`) as the container's `InitProcess`, so you don't need to launch a new process to reach it: after `session.CreateContainer(...)` and `container.Start()`, use the container's init `Process` handle to read its output streams (`GetOutputStream(ProcessOutputHandle.StandardOutput/StandardError)`) and observe exit/state. Note that writing to that process's stdin is currently broken — see #41091 (GetInputStream throws ArgumentException). If you can share what your container's `CMD` is and whether you need stdin vs. just stdout/stderr, we can point you at the exact calls.

To summarize where it stands for newcomers: WSLg draws server-side (RDP RAIL) decorations for the title bar, so an app's own GTK_THEME does not control the titlebar color; apps that draw client-side decorations (many GTK apps) follow the theme, while apps that rely on the WM/server titlebar (JetBrains, xcalc) get the default light frame. This is tagged help-wanted — a PR that plumbs a dark-titlebar preference (or honors the host's dark-mode / a `.wslgconfig` setting) through the RAIL decoration path would be very welcome. issues.title: WSLg PulseAudio connects successfully but audio streams time out (paplay) and microphone capture produces empty recordings
issues.next_action_reason: Repro and logs supplied; acknowledge the isolated data-channel diagnosis, ask about host-side audio config, keep audio-redirection label.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @EnZa666 — the diagnosis is thorough: control-plane works (pactl info connects, RDPSink/RDPSource enumerate) but every stream operation times out (paplay 'Stream error: Timeout', parecord writes a 44-byte header-only WAV, pactl suspend-sink also times out). That pattern says the PulseAudio↔RDP data channel to the Windows host isn't flowing even though the control socket is up. To help us correlate: does Windows show any audio activity for the WSLg session, and do you have a non-default audio setup on the host (exclusive-mode device, virtual audio driver, or a group-policy/enterprise audio restriction)? We have your WslLogs zip and will look at the RDP audio-redirection channel.
